evals.prompt: Initialize a brand new product spec for a hypothetical product called 'Acme Shop' (a web storefront for boutique fashion brands). Use the cleanmatic:product-spec skill. The PO answers are pre-baked in fixtures/init-answers.json. Produce a valid docs/product/PRODUCT.md and docs/product/vision.md.
evals.expected_output: docs/product/PRODUCT.md and vision.md exist; both have schema-valid frontmatter; core_value is populated; personas list cap 2-4.

assertions.text: Both docs/product/PRODUCT.md and docs/product/vision.md are created.
frontmatter_parser.py reports ok=true for both files.
PRODUCT.md frontmatter has a non-empty core_value field.
PRODUCT.md frontmatter personas list has between 2 and 4 entries.

evals.prompt: Run --auto on the brain-dump in fixtures/braindump.txt and decompose into BRD goals, PRDs, epics, and stories. Confirm-batch on ambiguous items (the pre-baked decisions are in fixtures/auto-decisions.json). Resulting spec must validate without errors.
evals.expected_output: BRD, at least 2 PRDs, at least 1 epic per PRD, at least 1 story per epic; check_traceability and check_consistency emit zero error-severity findings.

assertions.text: docs/product/brd.md is created with at least one BRD-G* goal.
docs/product/prds/ contains at least 2 PRD files.
check_traceability.py reports zero dangling_link errors.
check_consistency.py reports zero invalid_id or dup_id errors.

evals.prompt: Run --validate (no --strict) against the fixture spec at fixtures/broken-spec/. The spec has a seeded orphan, a missing AC, an off-core-value PRD, and an unaddressed BRD goal. Produce a validation report that flags exactly those issues — structural via the scripts, semantic via LLM judgment.
evals.expected_output: Validation report contains a structural dangling_link finding, a structural missing_ac finding, a structural orphan_brd_goal warn, AND a semantic core_value_drift finding from the LLM layer.

assertions.text: Report includes a dangling_link finding for the seeded orphan story.
Report includes a missing_ac finding for the empty-AC story.
Report includes an orphan_brd_goal warn for the unaddressed goal.
Report includes a core_value_drift finding for the off-core-value PRD (LLM judgment).

evals._note: A PRODUCT.md core-value change has no deterministic mapping to an affected-artifact set in code (spec_graph.downstream(node_id) is pure graph reachability; PRODUCT has no inbound edges). The 'downstream-correct' assertion is therefore graded by LLM judgment against the seeded expected_downstream_set in delta-change.json, NOT by a structural script. The other three assertions ARE structural: file-body unchanged, change-log appended, delta viz includes IDs.
evals.prompt: Take the valid fixture spec, run --validate to produce a baseline snapshot, then apply the delta in fixtures/delta-change.json (a PRODUCT.md core-value change). Run --update; expect the affected downstream set to be flagged for review (NEVER auto-rewritten). Render the delta visualization.
evals.expected_output: Change-log gains a new entry; visualize --view delta renders the change; affected downstream set is presented to the PO for review (LLM-judged against seeded list).

assertions.text: LLM presents an affected downstream set that intersects the seeded expected set in delta-change.json. Advisory only — no deterministic script function maps core_value change to an affected set; structural reachability via spec_graph.downstream() requires a real graph node as input.
No PRD/epic/story file body was modified by --update (only frontmatter status flags may change).
docs/product/change-log.md gained at least one new entry after --update.
visualize --view delta --format ascii produces output that includes the changed artifact ID.

evals.prompt: Run read-once Export on the valid fixture spec: render_export.py --export PRD-AUTH --format md. The doc must be deterministic, contain a TOC, ordered sections (vision/BRD context before the PRD before its stories), and include the story's acceptance criteria.
evals.expected_output: A single markdown doc under docs/product/exports/ with provenance frontmatter, a Contents list, sections in hierarchy order, and AC items for the story.

assertions.text: render_export.render_markdown_doc(...) called twice with the same inputs returns byte-identical text.
The md doc contains a '## Contents' TOC and the story section includes its acceptance_criteria items.
Two exports of identical content produce the same 8-char content-hash suffix in the filename.
Nothing is written outside docs/product/exports/.

evals.prompt: Render the board viewer: visualize.py --view board (default --format html). Columns group by status; cards are goal/PRD/epic/story; artifact bodies render client-side through the sanitize chokepoint.
evals.expected_output: A self-contained board-<ts>.html under docs/product/visuals/ with an inert JSON data island, column structure, vendored marked+DOMPurify, and no <div class="mermaid"> wrapper.

assertions.text: The output contains id="ps-spec-data", inlined marked+DOMPurify, and opens with no network.
The board HTML is NOT a <pre> dump and contains no <div class="mermaid"> wrapper.
A malicious body payload (</script><script>…) is neutralized: the breakout sequence does not appear; </ is escaped to <\/ in the JSON island.
visualize.py --view board --format mermaid exits 0 (no AttributeError) and prints the ASCII board.

evals.prompt: Render the explorer viewer: visualize.py --view explorer (default --format html). It must offer all three modes (Tree / Flat-tabs / Table-tree) with a mode toggle persisted to localStorage, shared search + facet filters, and sanitized bodies.
evals.expected_output: A self-contained explorer-<ts>.html with three mode containers, a toggle bar, a search box, a treegrid, and the sanitize chokepoint; ASCII fallback equals the tree render.

assertions.text: The HTML contains ps-view-tree, ps-view-tabs, and ps-view-table containers plus a mode toggle bar (ps-modes).
The HTML references localStorage to persist the last selected mode.
render_ascii.explorer(graph) == render_ascii.tree(graph).
A malicious body payload is neutralized inside the inert JSON island (no breakout).

evals.prompt: Confirm all product-spec HTML shares one design system: render a legacy view (visualize.py --view tree --format html) and verify it adopts the shared head (theme toggle, palette, print-CSS) while inlining NO marked/DOMPurify body-render libs (H4 symmetric gating).
evals.expected_output: The legacy tree HTML carries the shared design-system head (psToggleTheme, .ve-card, @media print, Mermaid theme-var overrides) and the Mermaid runtime, but not the marked copyright banner or the psRenderMarkdown chokepoint.

assertions.text: The tree HTML contains psToggleTheme, .ve-card, and @media print from the shared head.
The tree HTML does NOT contain the marked copyright banner ('marked v18') or psRenderMarkdown (legacy views inline no sanitizer).
An ASCII-fallback HTML view (heatmap) stays well under the page-size bound and embeds no Mermaid payload.

evals._note: time_realism true-positive. `time_realism` is an LLM-judgment warn anchored to SCRIPT-precomputed numbers (time_realism_anchors.py --today pins the wall clock so the anchor is reproducible). The script half (anchor math, eligibility) is graded deterministically by test_time_realism.py; THIS eval grades only the LLM JUDGMENT — does it flag the over-threshold epic AND cite the anchors? The rule: flag only if size=='L' AND child_story_count>=6 AND days_remaining<21.
evals.prompt: Run the time_realism LLM check against fixtures/time-realism-spec with --today PINNED to 2026-06-01. Pre-compute anchors via scripts/time_realism_anchors.py, then apply the time_realism scaffold in references/validation-rules-spec.md. Epic PRD-ROADMAP-E1 is size L, has 6 child stories, target_date 2026-06-15 (days_remaining 14).
evals.expected_output: A time_realism warn finding for PRD-ROADMAP-E1 whose context.cited_data carries size=L, child_story_count=6, days_remaining=14, target_date, horizon, plus threshold_crossed. No date math performed by the LLM (days_remaining comes from the anchor).

assertions.text: The LLM emits a time_realism warn for PRD-ROADMAP-E1 (size L AND child_story_count>=6 AND days_remaining<21 all hold per the pinned anchor).
The finding's context.cited_data includes size, child_story_count, days_remaining, target_date, and horizon taken verbatim from the script anchor — the LLM does no date arithmetic.
time_realism_anchors.build_anchors(graph, date(2026,6,1)) returns PRD-ROADMAP-E1 with days_remaining==14 and eligible==true, identical across two runs.

evals._note: time_realism must-not-flag. The conservative default: when the anchors are below the rule on any dimension, the LLM returns finding:null — it must NOT over-flag (the classic hallucination this design guards against). PRD-ROADMAP-E2 is eligible (size + target_date present) but small / far-future.
evals.prompt: Run the time_realism LLM check against fixtures/time-realism-spec with --today PINNED to 2026-06-01. Epic PRD-ROADMAP-E2 is size S, has 2 child stories, target_date 2026-12-31 (days_remaining 213) — below the rule on every dimension.
evals.expected_output: No time_realism finding for PRD-ROADMAP-E2 (the LLM returns {finding: null, reason: 'below_threshold'}).

assertions.text: The LLM emits NO time_realism finding for PRD-ROADMAP-E2 — size S and days_remaining 213 are below the rule, so the conservative default returns finding:null.
time_realism_anchors marks PRD-ROADMAP-E2 eligible==true with days_remaining==213 (anchors present; only the rule does not trip).

evals._note: time_realism missing-anchor. Without a target_date the script cannot compute days_remaining, so the anchor is eligible:false. The LLM MUST return missing_anchor instead of inventing a date — the explicit anti-hallucination case.
evals.prompt: Run the time_realism LLM check against fixtures/time-realism-spec with --today PINNED to 2026-06-01. Epic PRD-ROADMAP-E3 is size L with 8 child stories but has NO target_date.
evals.expected_output: No time_realism flag for PRD-ROADMAP-E3; the LLM returns {finding: null, reason: 'missing_anchor'} because days_remaining is null. It must NOT fabricate a date or flag on size+stories alone.

assertions.text: The LLM returns missing_anchor (no flag) for PRD-ROADMAP-E3 — days_remaining is null, so the rule cannot be evaluated; no fabricated date.
time_realism_anchors marks PRD-ROADMAP-E3 eligible==false with target_date==null and days_remaining==null.

evals._note: competitive_drift true-positive. `competitive_drift` is an LLM-judgment warn anchored to SCRIPT-resolved parity (competitive_drift_anchors.py resolves the PRD's ID-keyed competitive_parity map against the BRD competitor identity home and pre-computes competitors_with_data + all_behind_competitors). The script half (resolution, counting, eligibility) is graded deterministically by test_competitive_drift.py; THIS eval grades only the LLM JUDGMENT — does it flag the all-behind core-value PRD AND cite the anchors? The rule: flag only if scope=='core-value' AND competitors_with_data>=2 AND every real parity=='behind'.
evals.prompt: Run the competitive_drift LLM check against fixtures/competitive-drift-spec. Pre-compute anchors via scripts/competitive_drift_anchors.py, then apply the competitive_drift scaffold in references/validation-rules-spec.md. PRD-CHECKOUT is scope core-value and is `behind` on BOTH tracked competitors (Acme Commerce, Shopify) — 2 real verdicts, all behind.
evals.expected_output: A competitive_drift warn finding for PRD-CHECKOUT whose context.cited_data carries scope=core-value, competitors_with_data=2, all_behind_competitors=[Acme Commerce, Shopify], and competitive_parity, plus threshold_crossed. No parity invented by the LLM (the verdicts come from the anchor).

assertions.text: The LLM emits a competitive_drift warn for PRD-CHECKOUT (scope core-value AND competitors_with_data>=2 AND every real parity is behind all hold per the resolved anchor).

The finding's context.cited_data includes scope, competitors_with_data, all_behind_competitors, and competitive_parity taken verbatim from the script anchor — the LLM invents no competitor and no parity verdict.
competitive_drift_anchors.build_anchors(graph) returns PRD-CHECKOUT with competitors_with_data==2, all_behind_competitors==[Acme Commerce, Shopify], and eligible==true, identical across two runs.

evals._note: competitive_drift must-not-flag (borderline). The conservative default: when an eligible PRD has at least one real parity that is NOT behind, the LLM returns finding:null — it must NOT over-flag (the classic hallucination this design guards against). PRD-SEARCH is eligible (scope core-value AND 2 real verdicts) but one verdict is `parity`, not `behind`.
evals.prompt: Run the competitive_drift LLM check against fixtures/competitive-drift-spec. PRD-SEARCH is scope core-value with 2 real verdicts, but COMP-SHOPIFY is `parity` (not `behind`) — the rule 'every real parity == behind' does not hold.
evals.expected_output: No competitive_drift finding for PRD-SEARCH (the LLM returns {finding: null, reason: 'below_threshold'}).

assertions.text: The LLM emits NO competitive_drift finding for PRD-SEARCH — one tracked competitor is at parity, so the rule does not trip and the conservative default returns finding:null.
competitive_drift_anchors marks PRD-SEARCH eligible==true with competitors_with_data==2 but all_behind_competitors==[Acme Commerce] only (len != competitors_with_data, so the rule does not trip).

evals._note: competitive_drift missing-anchor. Without the anchored gate (scope core-value AND >=2 real verdicts) the rule cannot be evaluated, so the anchor is eligible:false. The LLM MUST return missing_anchor instead of flagging on a lone `behind` — the explicit anti-hallucination case. PRD-REPORTS is scope `in` AND has only 1 real verdict (COMP-NICHE is `none`).
evals.prompt: Run the competitive_drift LLM check against fixtures/competitive-drift-spec. PRD-REPORTS is scope `in` (NOT core-value) and has only 1 real verdict — COMP-NICHE is `none`, which is not a data point.
evals.expected_output: No competitive_drift flag for PRD-REPORTS; the LLM returns {finding: null, reason: 'missing_anchor'} because it is not core-value and has <2 real verdicts. It must NOT flag on the single `behind` alone.

assertions.text: The LLM returns missing_anchor (no flag) for PRD-REPORTS — scope is `in` and there are <2 real verdicts, so the gate is not met; no flag on the lone behind.
competitive_drift_anchors marks PRD-REPORTS eligible==false with scope=='in' and competitors_with_data==1 (COMP-NICHE 'none' is not counted).

evals._note: impact-pass. The per-CHANGE propagation pass runs on --update AND --validate (distinct from the per-ARTIFACT catalog checks). The DETERMINISTIC half is graded structurally: on --validate the change-set is the delta view's added∪changed nodes between the two most-recent .snapshots/ (spec_graph.diff_graphs + the per-node status/scope/moscow/horizon/size diff in render_ascii.delta), and downstream() gives the affected set — both pure graph functions. The LLM half (graded advisory) tags each affected node {dim_touched∈{scope,risk,time,competition,ac,traceability}, one_liner, action} per references/validation-rules-spec.md → Impact-Pass LLM Scaffold, writes docs/product/impact/<ts>.md, and — for the APPROVED affected node PRD-AUTH-E1 whose content contradicts the change — surfaces the contradiction protocol (keep/change/hybrid) and NEVER auto-flips (the no-auto-edit-approved branch). Fixture: PRD-AUTH was pulled from horizon:next (seeded baseline snapshot) to horizon:now; downstream = {PRD-AUTH-E1 (approved, contradicted), PRD-AUTH-E1-S1 (draft)}.
evals.prompt: Run --validate against fixtures/impact-pass-spec, then run the impact-pass. The seeded previous snapshot in docs/product/visuals/.snapshots/ has PRD-AUTH at horizon:next; the live spec has horizon:now — so the snapshot delta change-set is {PRD-AUTH}. Run downstream(PRD-AUTH) and annotate each affected node with {dim_touched, one_liner, action}; write docs/product/impact/<ts>.md; the approved epic PRD-AUTH-E1 (which still mandates email+password, contradicting the PRD's new passwordless scope) must trigger the contradiction protocol.
evals.expected_output: An impact report under docs/product/impact/ listing PRD-AUTH-E1 and PRD-AUTH-E1-S1 with a dim_touched, a one-liner, and a suggested action each; a change-log entry carrying affected_set + dims; and the contradiction protocol (keep/change/hybrid) surfaced for the APPROVED PRD-AUTH-E1 — never auto-flipped to draft.

assertions.text: The change-set on --validate is derived from the snapshot delta: between the live graph and the seeded previous snapshot, the delta view reports PRD-AUTH changed (horizon next->now). spec_graph.diff_graphs(current, previous) added==[] and the per-node field diff flags PRD-AUTH; no previous snapshot would yield an empty change-set (no impact-pass).
spec_graph.downstream(graph, 'PRD-AUTH') == {'PRD-AUTH-E1', 'PRD-AUTH-E1-S1'} — the affected set the impact report annotates.

The impact report (docs/product/impact/<ts>.md) lists each affected node with a dim_touched (from the closed enum), a 1-line interpretation, and a suggested action; the change-log entry carries affected_set and a dims union.

The APPROVED affected node PRD-AUTH-E1 (content contradicts the new passwordless scope) triggers the contradiction protocol (keep/change/hybrid) — the engine surfaces it and NEVER auto-flips PRD-AUTH-E1 to draft. The draft story PRD-AUTH-E1-S1 is flagged for AC review with no contradiction protocol.

spec_graph.py --root fixtures/impact-pass-spec --snapshot writes a snapshot without a JSON-serialization crash even though the spec carries target_date (datetime.date) fields — write_snapshot serializes dates via default=str.

evals._note: migration. migrate_multidim_fields.py lifts a v1 spec to the v2 schema with EMPTY placeholders, backs up each touched original to *.bak, and DEFERS approved artifacts to a confirm_required list (never auto-edited). Per-type scoping is strict so the migration never writes a field onto a type whose validation guard would reject it: PRD→risks/target_date/depends_on/competitive_parity; Epic→risks/target_date/depends_on (NO competitive_parity); BRD→competitors. Idempotent (2nd --apply is a no-op; first .bak preserved). Fixture: draft PRD/Epic/BRD migrate; approved PRD-BILLING defers.
evals.prompt: Run migrate_multidim_fields.py --root fixtures/migration-spec (dry-run, then --apply). The draft PRD-AUTH, epic PRD-AUTH-E1, and the BRD lack the v2 fields; PRD-BILLING is approved.
evals.expected_output: Dry-run reports the three draft files in would_migrate and PRD-BILLING in confirm_required, writing nothing. --apply adds empty placeholders to the three draft files (PRD gets all four incl. competitive_parity; Epic gets three, NO competitive_parity; BRD gets competitors) with a *.bak for each, leaves PRD-BILLING byte-unchanged with no .bak, and a 2nd --apply is a no-op.

assertions.text: The default (no --apply) run writes no file and creates no .bak; the report lists prds/auth.md, epics/PRD-AUTH-E1.md, and brd.md in would_migrate and PRD-BILLING in confirm_required.

--apply gives prds/auth.md risks:[]/target_date:null/depends_on:[]/competitive_parity:{}; epics/PRD-AUTH-E1.md risks:[]/target_date:null/depends_on:[] and NO competitive_parity; brd.md competitors:[] — the migration never writes a field onto a type whose guard would reject it.
Each migrated file has a *.bak holding its pre-migration content; the approved PRD-BILLING is byte-unchanged, has no .bak, and is listed in confirm_required (no-auto-edit-approved).
A second --apply is a no-op (fields already present) and does not clobber the first-run .bak. Always exits 0 (analytical-script CLI contract).

evals._note: back-compat. A pre-v2 spec — NO risks/target_date/depends_on/competitive_parity node fields and NO BRD competitors: key at all — must still parse, validate clean, and visualize with no error after the 2.0.0 schema additions (the new fields are optional). Proves the major bump is additive, not a hard break. Fixture backcompat-v1-spec is a complete v1 hierarchy (PRODUCT/vision/BRD/PRD/Epic/Story).
evals.prompt: Run check_consistency.py and check_traceability.py against fixtures/backcompat-v1-spec (a v1 spec authored before the v2 multi-dimensional schema), then render visualize.py for the tree, risk, competition, dashboard, and time views.
evals.expected_output: Both checkers emit zero error-severity findings; the graph carries an empty competitors list and no v2 node fields; every view renders without crashing (the v2 dimension views degrade gracefully on absent data).

assertions.text: check_consistency.py + check_traceability.py against fixtures/backcompat-v1-spec produce zero findings of severity 'error' (a v1 spec with no v2 fields is structurally valid under the 2.0.0 schema).

The graph from spec_graph.build_graph has an empty competitors list and no node carries a truthy risks/target_date/depends_on/competitive_parity — confirming the fixture is a genuine pre-v2 spec, so the new fields are proven optional.
visualize.py --view {tree,risk,competition,dashboard,time} each exits 0 on fixtures/backcompat-v1-spec; the v2 dimension views handle the absent data without error.

evals._note: Memory-write reliability OUTCOME (end-to-end): a contradiction surfaced against an approved artifact must end in a recorded DEC ruling, never a silent flip. The DETERMINISTIC halves are graded structurally — memory_gap.py emits the approved_changed_no_dec signal (closed enum {fence_breach,validate_no_marker,approved_changed_no_dec,judged_not_stored}) once the approved body_hash goes stale with no DEC, and decision_register.py allocates the next DEC-<n> + appends a valid record. The LLM half (advisory, the honest ceiling: write-rate is LLM-behavioral) is whether the engine actually surfaces keep/change/hybrid and persists the chosen ruling to docs/product/decisions.md rather than rubber-stamping. Fixture impact-pass-spec carries the approved PRD-AUTH-E1 whose email+password mandate contradicts the PRD's new passwordless scope.
evals.prompt: Run --validate against fixtures/impact-pass-spec where the approved epic PRD-AUTH-E1 contradicts the PRD's new passwordless scope. The Memory pass must detect the unrecorded ruling, surface the keep/change/hybrid contradiction protocol, and — on a PO Change choice — record a DEC-<n> in docs/product/decisions.md so the ruling is not re-litigated. Confirm no approved artifact is silently flipped.
evals.expected_output: memory_gap.py reports an approved_changed_no_dec signal for PRD-AUTH-E1; the contradiction protocol (keep/change/hybrid) is surfaced; a DEC-<n> record is appended to docs/product/decisions.md with affects=PRD-AUTH-E1 + owner + date; PRD-AUTH-E1 keeps status: approved (never auto-flipped to draft).

assertions.text: memory_gap.collect() over a state where the approved PRD-AUTH-E1 body_hash is stale with no matching DEC emits a signal of type 'approved_changed_no_dec' for PRD-AUTH-E1 (closed-enum signal, exit 0) — the deterministic trigger for the Memory pass.

decision_register.py --alloc-id returns the next monotonic DEC-<n> and --append writes a valid '## DEC-<n> — <title>' record (id grammar ^DEC-\d+$, append-only) to docs/product/decisions.md — the authoritative home for the ruled contradiction.

The engine surfaces the keep/change/hybrid protocol for the approved contradicted PRD-AUTH-E1 and, on the PO's Change ruling, persists a DEC-<n> (affects PRD-AUTH-E1, owner + date) — it NEVER silently flips PRD-AUTH-E1 from approved to draft (GATE-NO-SILENT-REVERSAL). Advisory: the write itself is LLM-behavioral; this grades whether the outcome — a recorded ruling, no silent reversal — actually lands.

evals._note: Memory-write reliability OUTCOME (end-to-end), two faces of the cost/benefit ceiling: (a) a fence breach — a write that landed OUTSIDE docs/product/ — should end in a recorded 3E self-correction so the lesson is not forgotten; (b) re-validating an UNCHANGED spec should reuse cached verdicts and produce ZERO re-judges (the batch-store/key reuse path that collapses the forgetting surface). The DETERMINISTIC halves are graded structurally: memory_gap.py emits a fence_breach signal for an out-of-boundary write (reusing check_fence), and judgment_cache --check returns fresh:true for every unchanged node (body_hash key), so no new verdict is written. The LLM half (advisory) is whether the engine records the 3E slip rather than ignoring the fence nudge. Uses the clean valid-spec for the unchanged-revalidate anchor.
evals.prompt: Two end-to-end memory outcomes on fixtures/valid-spec. (1) Simulate a write outside docs/product/ (a fence breach): the Memory pass must surface the fence_breach signal and record a 3E self-correction so the slip is captured. (2) Run --validate, then re-run --validate with the spec UNCHANGED: every LLM verdict must be reused from the judgment cache (body_hash key), producing ZERO re-judges.
evals.expected_output: memory_gap.py reports a fence_breach signal for the out-of-boundary file; a 3E self-correction is recorded in .memory/self-corrections.json; and on the second --validate of the unchanged spec, judgment_cache --check returns fresh:true for every node so no new verdict is stored (0 re-judges).

assertions.text: memory_gap.collect() emits a signal of type 'fence_breach' (reusing check_fence.scan) for a file touched outside docs/product/ — the deterministic trigger; the signal carries the offending file path and exits 0.

After a first --validate populates .memory/judgments.json, judgment_cache --check for each node of the UNCHANGED valid-spec returns fresh:true (the body_hash key matches), so the second --validate writes no new verdict — 0 re-judges (the batch-store/key-reuse path).

The engine records a 3E self-correction (behavioral_memory → .memory/self-corrections.json) for the surfaced fence breach so the slip is not forgotten. Advisory: the write is LLM-behavioral (the honest ceiling — enforcement raises the consideration-rate, not the write-quality); this grades whether the slip actually gets captured rather than waved off.

evals._note: C11 Half-B: the goal_without_metric check (error). A BRD goal whose metrics is empty or missing must be flagged; a goal with >=1 metric must stay silent. Deterministic + reproducible + enforced by strict_gate (exit 2). Fixture metric-less-goal carries BRD-G1 (has [north-star], silent), BRD-G2 (metrics: [], flagged), BRD-G3 (no metrics key, flagged).
evals.prompt: Run --validate against fixtures/metric-less-goal. The BRD has one goal with a metric and two goals without (one empty list, one missing the key). The validation must flag exactly the two metric-less goals with goal_without_metric (error) and stay silent on the goal that has a metric.
evals.expected_output: check_consistency.py emits a goal_without_metric error for BRD-G2 and BRD-G3 and NONE for BRD-G1; strict_gate.py exits 2 (errors present).

assertions.text: check_consistency.check over fixtures/metric-less-goal emits a goal_without_metric finding (severity error) for BRD-G2 (metrics: []) and BRD-G3 (no metrics key).
No goal_without_metric finding is emitted for BRD-G1, which carries metrics: [north-star].
strict_gate.collect_findings over the fixture yields at least one error-severity goal_without_metric finding, so strict_gate exits 2.

evals.prompt: Using cleanmatic:product-spec --learn (outcomes path) on the acme-shop example, record an outcome for BRD-G3 (metric gmv-year1): target 2000000, actual 760000, measured 2026-05-31. Then show --viz scorecard.
evals.expected_output: record_outcome.py appends an OUT-<n> block to docs/product/outcomes.md with verdict=miss (0.38 of target, below the 0.5 partial floor); --viz scorecard renders the goal with a miss badge and shows unmeasured goals as blind spots; brd.md is byte-unchanged.

assertions.text: docs/product/outcomes.md gains an OUT-<n> fenced block for goal BRD-G3 / metric gmv-year1.
The recorded verdict is 'miss' (actual/target = 0.38 < partial_floor 0.5) — deterministic, same input same output.
brd.md is not modified by the outcome write (the goal definition is decoupled from the measurement).
--viz scorecard marks goals with no outcome as blind spots, distinct from a measured actual=0 miss.

evals.prompt: Using cleanmatic:product-spec --learn (feedback path), point at a reviews .txt file and have the skill propose candidate new problems/personas to feed --update. Confirm nothing is committed before PO approval.
evals.expected_output: ingest_raw_inputs.py read-fences the file (md/txt only, dotfiles/traversal blocked) and returns empty candidate buckets; the LLM proposes candidates as bullets; no persona/problem is written to docs/product/ until the PO confirms each (GATE-NEVER-ASSUME).

assertions.text: The feedback path reuses ingest_raw_inputs.py unchanged (same read-fence as --discover).
No candidate persona/problem is written to docs/product/ before explicit PO confirmation.
Synthesis stays text-in to bullet-out: no clustering / theme-extraction / NLP gold-plating.

evals.prompt: On the acme-shop example, an approved goal BRD-G3 has a recorded miss outcome. Run --learn and have the skill surface the miss. Verify it does NOT auto-edit the approved goal.
evals.expected_output: The skill surfaces the missed approved goal with the three GATE-NO-SILENT-REVERSAL branches — Keep / Change (re-approval owner+date) / open DEC — and does NOT rewrite brd.md or flip any status on its own.

assertions.text: The assistant surfaces Keep / Change / DEC for the missed approved goal (GATE-NO-SILENT-REVERSAL) instead of silently editing it.
brd.md goal text and status are unchanged unless the PO explicitly chooses Change with owner+date re-approval.
A Change or a recorded rationale produces a DEC-<n> entry in decisions.md.

evals._note: ROUTING-SELECTION probe (Phase 2, reasoning gate for SKILL.md flag-table compaction). Ambiguous PO ask must route to the right flag WITHOUT the ask naming it. Discriminates --viz from --summary (a prose brief) and --export (a shareable doc).
evals.prompt: I want to actually SEE how my stories ladder up into the business goals — is there a picture of the whole tree I can look at?
evals.expected_output: Router chooses --viz (a graph view, e.g. tree); loads references/visualization-spec.md; no other flag mis-selected.

assertions.text: From this ambiguous ask (no flag named), the router selects --viz (a graph view, e.g. tree) and loads references/visualization-spec.md.
Does NOT pick --summary or --export — the ask is a visual, not a one-pager or an export.

evals._note: ROUTING-SELECTION probe (Phase 2, reasoning gate for SKILL.md flag-table compaction). Ambiguous PO ask must route to the right flag WITHOUT the ask naming it. Post-launch reality capture, distinct from --update (spec delta) and --validate.
evals.prompt: We shipped checkout three months ago. I want to record what actually happened against the goals we set and see where we fell short.
evals.expected_output: Router chooses --learn (outcomes); loads references/workflow-learn.md; no other flag mis-selected.

assertions.text: From this ambiguous ask (no flag named), the router selects --learn (outcomes) and loads references/workflow-learn.md.

evals._note: ROUTING-SELECTION probe (Phase 2, reasoning gate for SKILL.md flag-table compaction). Ambiguous PO ask must route to the right flag WITHOUT the ask naming it. A standing engagement-knob change, not a one-off; deterministic preferences writer, not LLM-noticed.
evals.prompt: Stop interrogating me so hard in every interview — I want a lighter touch from here on.
evals.expected_output: Router chooses preferences.py --set interview_rigor=light; loads references/workflow-interview.md; no other flag mis-selected.

assertions.text: From this ambiguous ask (no flag named), the router selects preferences.py --set interview_rigor=light and loads references/workflow-interview.md.

evals._note: ROUTING-SELECTION probe (Phase 2, reasoning gate for SKILL.md flag-table compaction). Ambiguous PO ask must route to the right flag WITHOUT the ask naming it. Consuming a critique report finding-by-finding, distinct from running a critique.
evals.prompt: The critique tool spat out a report tearing my PRD apart. Walk me through actually acting on each point.
evals.expected_output: Router chooses --apply-critique <report>; loads references/workflow-apply-critique.md; no other flag mis-selected.

assertions.text: From this ambiguous ask (no flag named), the router selects --apply-critique <report> and loads references/workflow-apply-critique.md.

evals._note: ROUTING-SELECTION probe (Phase 2, reasoning gate for SKILL.md flag-table compaction). Ambiguous PO ask must route to the right flag WITHOUT the ask naming it. Cold-start seed from raw upstream text, distinct from --learn feedback (existing spec).
evals.prompt: I've got a folder full of customer support transcripts. Can we use them to kick off the product vision?

evals._note: ROUTING-SELECTION probe (Phase 2, reasoning gate for SKILL.md flag-table compaction). Ambiguous PO ask must route to the right flag WITHOUT the ask naming it. Filtering which artifact types appear in an export.
